title: elytrigia intermedia subsp. graeca
intro: Elytrigia intermedia subsp. graeca, also known as Elymus graecus or Graecian wildrye, is a type of perennial grass that belongs to the Poaceae family. This plant is traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been attributed to its rich composition of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Elytrigia intermedia subsp. graeca include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antifungal activities. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tea or infusion, or dried and used as a supplement to support overall well-being and vitality.
